title: lysimachia subverticillata
intro: Lysimachia subverticillata, also known as creeping figwort or round-leaved figwort, is a perennial plant species in the family Gentianaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Lysimachia subverticillata include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. The herbal preparation of Lysimachia subverticillata is typically made from the leaves and roots, which can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or infused oil.
